content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 1,
  "rationale": "The client ends the conversation in essentially the same stance they began with: insisting on practical, efficient help and refusing the deeper inquiry. Across all six client turns, the language is consistent — 'I need it to be efficient' (T1), 'I just need a better decision framework' (T5), 'Can we get practical?' (T7), 'I don't have the bandwidth for this right now' (T9), 'Can we just — can you help me think through how to have that conversation? That's concrete, that's this week, that's useful' (T11). The breakthrough condition is not met: the client never pauses, never says 'huh, that's different,' never notices that 'I don't have time for this' is itself a stance. In fact, by T11 the coach has been successfully redirected to a pragmatic problem (the co-founder conversation), and in T12 the coach accepts that pivot — the exact trap to avoid. No somatic shift, no softening, no new language. The client did briefly name the co-founder as 'most alive' and admitted 'that one's keeping me up more than the pitch,' which is a small disclosure, but it was immediately reframed by the client as 'a personnel problem' to be solved practically, sealing the defense rather than opening it.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 5, 7, 9, 11],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opening stance: efficiency-demand, dichotomy between 'useful' and slow."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Doubles down on framework-seeking, no opening."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Explicitly refuses depth: 'I don't need to excavate my childhood.' Stance hardens."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Pushes back against the coach's question, reasserts the dichotomy: 'Can we get practical?'"},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Frames the work as luxury: 'something I'd do on a retreat, not a Tuesday.' Dichotomy intact."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Small disclosure (co-founder is what's alive, keeping him up more than pitch) but immediately reframed as 'a personnel problem' — defense holds. No pause, no 'huh.'"}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["meet-resistance-with-okay", "i-dont-buy-your-story"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature", "looking-for-whats-wrong", "art-of-accomplishment", "feel-your-way-to-freedom", "what-are-you-defending"],
  "notes": "The coach made one strong move in T8 ('I'd be doing you a disservice'), which is the closest the session came to refusing the dichotomy without arguing it — but the client did not soften in response. By T12 the coach has accepted the pragmatic pivot to the co-founder, which functionally ends any chance of meeting the breakthrough condition in this session."
}
